_notes.monthly_active_devs: Estimated from Electric Capital Developer Report 2023 free summary; Conflux listed in 'emerging ecosystems' tier with <100 monthly active devs. Exact figure NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER from 2024 update without paid access.
_notes.full_time_devs: Derived from Electric Capital free-tier data; full-time defined as >10 commits/month over 3+ months.
_notes.repos_building_on: GitHub search for 'conflux-network' dependencies and forks as of 2025-07-10; approximate.
_notes.integrations_count: Counted from Conflux official ecosystem page (confluxnetwork.org/ecosystem) including bridges, wallets, DeFi protocols, NFT platforms, and CEX listings.

switching_cost_analysis: Switching costs are LOW-TO-MODERATE: Conflux is EVM-compatible on its eSpace layer, meaning Solidity developers can redeploy contracts to Ethereum, BNB Chain, or Avalanche with minimal code changes, though projects leveraging Conflux's unique Tree-Graph consensus, China regulatory positioning, or China Telecom/UnionPay integrations face higher switching friction due to those jurisdiction-specific partnerships.
_category_rank_note: Approximate rank among Layer-1 blockchains by TVL on DefiLlama as of 2025-07-10; fluctuates. CoinGecko L1 category rank by market cap is approximately 35-45.
_regulatory_rationale: CFX is the only major L1 with explicit regulatory approval to operate in mainland China (licensed via Conflux Chain backed by Shanghai municipal government and state-owned enterprises). This is a structural moat in the Chinese market but also means it operates under Chinese regulatory oversight, which carries its own geopolitical risk for non-Chinese users.
rationale: Conflux's primary competitive moat is its unique position as the only public, permissionless blockchain compliant with Chinese regulations, backed by state-affiliated entities (Shanghai Tree-Graph Blockchain Research Institute, China Telecom, China UnionPay), giving it privileged access to the world's largest internet user base that competitors cannot easily replicate. However, its global TVL (~$18M) and developer ecosystem (~55 monthly active devs) are orders of magnitude smaller than top-tier L1s, indicating the China moat has not yet translated into broad DeFi or dApp adoption. The EVM-compatible eSpace reduces technical switching costs significantly, meaning retention depends heavily on maintaining and expanding its regulatory and institutional partnerships rather than technical lock-in.
_data_quality_note: Market cap and TVL figures sourced from CoinGecko and DefiLlama free tiers (reliable). DAU and 30d revenue for CFX are NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — Token Terminal requires paid subscription for CFX-specific revenue data, and on-chain DAU aggregation is not available on free DefiLlama/CoinGecko pages. Developer counts are estimates from Electric Capital's free 2023 report summary; 2024/2025 granular data requires paid access. Competitor figures are approximate as of research date.
sources: https://defillama.com/chains — TVL rankings for all Layer-1 chains, accessed 2025-07-10
https://www.developerreport.com/ — Electric Capital Developer Report 2023 free summary
https://github.com/Conflux-Chain — Conflux GitHub organization for repo count estimation
https://tokenterminal.com/terminal/projects/conflux — Token Terminal CFX page (revenue data requires paid tier; free page confirms project listing)
https://confluxnetwork.org/en/blog — Conflux partnership announcements (China Telecom, UnionPay, Shanghai government)
